GET /accounts/{account_id}/data-security.description: Returns a list of integration-scoped workflow configurations for the given account. This endpoint supports cursor based pagination. By default, results are returned in sorted order based on created_at.

parameters.description: Cloudflare account ID for the user making the request.
parameters.name: account_id

parameters.description: Cursor for pagination. Obtained from the `result_info.cursor` field of a previous response.
parameters.name: cursor

JSON pointer to the source of the error.

next.description: URL to the next page of results.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

previous.description: URL to the previous page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

items.description: Response body for a workflow configuration.

actions.description: The actions configured for this workflow.

remediation_types.description: List of remediation types that will be executed.

items.description: A remediation type configured for the workflow.

remediation_type.description: The system name of the remediation type.

remediation_type_id.description: Unique identifier for the remediation type.

webhook_configs.description: List of webhook configurations that will be triggered.

items.description: A webhook configuration associated with the workflow.

display_name.description: Display name/label of the webhook configuration.

webhook_config_id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook configuration.

applies_to_all_integrations.description: When true, the workflow applies to all integrations for the account. When false, it applies only to the specified integration_ids.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was created.

description.description: User-set description of what this workflow does. Limited to 1000 characters.
description.example: Automatically remove public access from files when detected

disabled_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was disabled. Omitted from the response when the workflow is enabled.

display_name.description: Display name for the workflow configuration. Limited to 255 characters.

finding_type_id.description: The finding type this workflow is associated with.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workflow configuration.

integration_ids.description: The integrations this workflow applies to.

last_triggered_at.description:
Timestamp of the most recent successful workflow invocation. Omitted
from the response when the workflow has never been successfully
triggered. Only populated on GET responses; absent on responses from
create/update endpoints.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was last updated.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/data-security.summary: Get a workflow configuration by ID

GET /accounts/{account_id}/data-security.description:
Returns a list of workflow invocation logs for the given account, sourced from ClickHouse via Ready Analytics. Each entry represents one execution of a workflow against a finding instance, including the actions (remediation jobs and webhooks) it dispatched.

Results are scoped to the requesting account's CASB integrations. The `integration_id` query parameter, when supplied, is intersected against the account's integration allowlist; values outside the allowlist are silently dropped.

Pagination is offset-based. The response does not include a total row count; clients should treat a result with fewer than `per_page` entries as the end of the list.

parameters.description: Cloudflare account ID for the user making the request.
parameters.name: account_id

parameters.description: Lower bound on the log entry timestamp. Accepts ISO 8601 / RFC3339 or epoch seconds. Defaults to 7 days before `until`. The window between `since` and `until` cannot exceed 180 days.
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Upper bound on the log entry timestamp. Accepts ISO 8601 / RFC3339 or epoch seconds. Defaults to the current time. Must be greater than or equal to `since`.
parameters.name: until

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of CASB integration IDs to filter by. At most 100 values, each at most 128 characters. Values outside the account's tenancy allowlist are silently ignored. When omitted, results are scoped to the full allowlist.
parameters.name: integration_id

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of workflow display names to filter by. At most 100 values, each at most 255 characters.
parameters.name: workflow_name

parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Number of results to return per page.
parameters.name: per_page

pointer.description: JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.

count.description: Total number of results for the requested service.

next.description: URL to the next page of results.

page.description: Current page within paginated list of results.

per_page.description: Number of results per page of results.

previous.description: URL to the previous page of results.

total_count.description: Total results available without any search parameters.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

items.description: A single workflow invocation log entry. Each entry represents one execution of a workflow against a finding instance.

actions.description: Actions dispatched by the workflow consumer for this invocation. Both arrays are always present and may be empty.

remediations.description: Remediation jobs spawned by this workflow invocation.

items.description: A remediation job dispatched during a workflow invocation.

id.description: Identifier of the remediation job, suitable for cross-referencing with the remediations API.

type.description: Display name of the remediation type that ran.

url.description: Path to the remediation job in the dashboard.

webhooks.description: Webhook jobs dispatched by this workflow invocation.

items.description: A webhook job dispatched during a workflow invocation.

nickname.description: User-set nickname of the webhook configuration that fired.

url.description: Path to the webhook configuration in the dashboard.

timestamp.description: When the workflow invocation was recorded by ClickHouse, in ClickHouse DateTime format (UTC).

trigger.description: The finding instance that caused the workflow to fire.

date_detected.description: When the finding was first detected, in ISO 8601 / RFC3339 format.

fields_of_interest.description: Free-form JSON object containing finding-type-specific context fields (e.g. file owner, sharing scope). Schema varies per finding type; consumers should treat unknown keys defensively. Null when the upstream row had no fields_of_interest.

finding_type_name.description: Display name of the finding type that triggered the workflow.

instance_asset.description: Name of the specific resource (file, drive item, etc.) the finding was raised against.

integration_name.description: Display name of the CASB integration that produced the finding.

integration_url.description: Path to the integration in the dashboard.

name.description: Display name of the workflow at the time it ran.

url.description: URL to the workflow configuration in the dashboard. Currently always an empty string; populated in a future revision once the upstream view exposes the workflow configuration ID.

pointer.description: JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/data-securit.description:
Creates a new workflow configuration that defines automated actions to be executed 
when security findings are detected. A workflow can include multiple remediation 
and/or webhook actions that will be triggered automatically.

parameters.description: Cloudflare account ID for the user making the request.
parameters.name: account_id

schema.description: Request body for creating a new workflow configuration.

actions.description:
List of actions to execute when this workflow is triggered. Each action can be either a remediation or a webhook.
A workflow must contain at least one action and may include at most one remediation action; webhook actions are unconstrained.

items.description: An action to be executed as part of a workflow. Must contain exactly one of remediation or webhook.

remediation.description: A remediation action to be executed.

remediation_type_id.description: The ID of the remediation type to execute.

webhook.description: A webhook action to be executed.

webhook_config_id.description: The ID of the webhook configuration to use.

applies_to_all_integrations.description: When true, the workflow applies to all integrations for the account. When false, integration_ids must be provided.

description.description: Optional description of what this workflow does.
description.example: Automatically remove public access from files when detected

display_name.description: Display name for the workflow configuration.

enabled.description: Boolean specifying if the workflow is enabled or disabled.

finding_type_id.description: The finding type this workflow is associated with. All remediation actions must match this finding type.

integration_ids.description: The integrations this workflow applies to. Required when applies_to_all_integrations is false.

allOf.description: Common response structure for all API endpoints.

pointer.description: JSON pointer to the source of the error.
JSON pointer to the source of the error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

result.description: Response body for a workflow configuration.

actions.description: The actions configured for this workflow.

remediation_types.description: List of remediation types that will be executed.

items.description: A remediation type configured for the workflow.

display_name.description: Display name/label of the remediation type.

remediation_type.description: The system name of the remediation type.

remediation_type_id.description: Unique identifier for the remediation type.

webhook_configs.description: List of webhook configurations that will be triggered.

items.description: A webhook configuration associated with the workflow.

display_name.description: Display name/label of the webhook configuration.

webhook_config_id.description: Unique identifier for the webhook configuration.

applies_to_all_integrations.description: When true, the workflow applies to all integrations for the account. When false, it applies only to the specified integration_ids.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was created.

description.description: User-set description of what this workflow does. Limited to 1000 characters.
description.example: Automatically remove public access from files when detected

disabled_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was disabled. Omitted from the response when the workflow is enabled.

display_name.description: Display name for the workflow configuration. Limited to 255 characters.

finding_type_id.description: The finding type this workflow is associated with.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workflow configuration.

integration_ids.description: The integrations this workflow applies to.

last_triggered_at.description:
Timestamp of the most recent successful workflow invocation. Omitted
from the response when the workflow has never been successfully
triggered. Only populated on GET responses; absent on responses from
create/update endpoints.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the workflow was last updated.
